# Phase 10 Runtime Certification Plan

## Objective

Define certification-readiness doctrine for future runtime-capable components without enabling runtime, providers, adapters, deployment, activation, or execution.

## Scope

- Runtime certification doctrine.
- Controlled activation readiness philosophy.
- Certification boundary framework.
- Federated execution safety.
- Certifiable module standards.
- Adapter and provider certification readiness.
- Shared-core protection.
- Human-certified activation prerequisites.
- Certification non-authorization.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- Provider onboarding.
- Adapter implementation.
- Credential creation.
- OAuth setup.
- Prisma or Supabase changes.
- Twilio or email execution.
- Background jobs.
- Autonomous certification engines.
- Automatic activation approval.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Certification is never authorization.
- Shared constitutional core remains protected.
- Industry modules cannot mutate the core.
- Adapter boundaries remain explicit.
- Provider readiness remains review-only.
- Federated entity sovereignty is preserved.
- Human review is required before any future activation review.
- Ambiguity fails closed.
